channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US East endures flooding, with snow in the forecast for the Northeast. Frigid cold in the Plains
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Much of the U.S. East endured a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather Saturday, with snowstorms predicted in the Northeast and heavy winds bringing the threat of tornadoes to the Mississippi Valley.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri community supports Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Migrant shelters nearly empty at Texas border
item.description: Illegal crossings have plummeted across the southern border to just an average of 600 per day after President Trump's crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, NewsNation

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KREX 5

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases over 300 Palestinian prisoners after receiving 3 more hostages
item.description: Israel and Hamas completed their sixth exchange of hostages and prisoners on Saturday after their ceasefire survived its latest crisis. The Palestinian militant group was going to delay the exchange because Israel wasn’t letting the agreed-upon humanitarian aid into the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tours Boeing plane to highlight aircraft maker's delay in delivering a new Air Force One
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump toured a Boeing airplane to check out new hardware and technology features and highlight the aircraft maker's delay in delivering updated versions of the Air Force One presidential aircraft, the White House said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Justice Department fires 20 immigration judges from backlogged courts amid major government cuts
item.description: The Trump administration fired 20 immigration judges without explanation, a union official said Saturday amid sweeping moves to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died after the car-ramming attack in Munich
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died two days after they were injured in the car-ramming attack on a labor union demonstration in Munich, police said Saturday. A 24-year-old Afghan man who came to Germany as an asylum-seeker was arrested immediately after the attack on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rodeo rider cheats death when bull's horn slices neck open
item.description: A fearless rodeo rider in Florida had a close brush with death when the bull he was riding gored him in the neck while being bucked off the beast’s back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment for a home is a challenge, especially in today’s housing market. But one persistent financial myth could be making it harder than it needs to be.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eastern U.S. braces for flooding while fire-damaged California city now faces mudslides
item.description: SIERRA MADRE, Calif. (AP) — Much of the Eastern U.S. braced for a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather on Saturday, with thunderstorms and melting snow combining to pound the Ohio and Tennessee valleys. The lower Mississippi valley was facing the threat of strong tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suzanne Simpson murder case: Texas defense for missing realtor's husband looking for holes in evidence trove
item.description: Suzanne Simpson vanished over four months ago, and her husband, charged with her murder, made an appearance in court, where attorneys discussed evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Andrew Lester pleads guilty in shooting of unarmed Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl
item.description: Andrew Lester pleaded guilty to second-degree assault after shooting 16-year-old Ralph Yarl outside Kansas City, Missouri home in April 2023
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Driver in Utah escapes car before train smashes into vehicle
item.description: Video appears to show that the SUV may have been pushed onto the tracks by another vehicle, just as the crossing gates were beginning to lower.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5
